message:
fix(codegen): Handle module attribute references vs calls correctly

When module attributes like abc.ABCMeta or abc.get_cache_token are used
as references (passed to functions) rather than called, the dispatch
handlers were incorrectly emitting call results instead of returning
UnsupportedSyntax to let the fallback emit proper references.

Changes:
- abc_mod.zig: genABCMeta and genGetCacheToken now return UnsupportedSyntax
  when args.len == 0, indicating reference usage not call usage
- _py_abc_mod.zig: Same fix for genGetCacheToken
- Runtime abc.zig: Added get_cache_token() function for ABC registry tracking
- Runtime _py_abc.zig: Added get_cache_token() for pure Python implementation

This fixes the missing comma bug where test_factory(allocator, "ABCMeta"&abc.ABCMeta)
was generated instead of test_factory(allocator, &abc.ABCMeta).
